steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: $f(m) = 21 - 3m$ gives the account balance after renting $m$ movies. The question asks for the fixed dollar amount withdrawn each time one movie is rented — that is, the magnitude of the rate of change per movie.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $3m$: This is an expression that grows with $m$, not a fixed per-rental amount. ✗
  • Choice B — $3$: The coefficient of $m$ is $-3$, meaning the balance drops by $3$ dollars per movie rented. A constant $3$ per rental is correct. ✓
  • Choice C — $21$: This is the initial balance (the $y$-intercept when $m=0$), not the per-rental cost. ✗
  • Choice D — $21-3m$: This is the entire expression for the balance, not the amount withdrawn per rental. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
